task_completion.description: Open the base rocket design in OpenRocket
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket application window is open with 'hpr_upgrade_base' visible in the title bar or the rocket design tree.

task_completion.description: Change the material of the Nose Cone and Body Tube to Fiberglass
task_completion.visual_evidence: Component configuration dialogs for the Nose Cone and Body Tube showing 'Fiberglass' selected in the Material dropdown.

task_completion.description: Change the material of the Fin set to Plywood or Birch
task_completion.visual_evidence: Component configuration dialog for the fins (e.g., Trapezoidal fin set) showing 'Plywood' or 'Birch' selected in the Material dropdown.

task_completion.description: Run a flight simulation to verify performance
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' tab in OpenRocket showing a completed simulation run with calculated metrics like Apogee, Max velocity, etc.

task_completion.description: Save the modified design as 'upgraded_rocket.ork' in ~/Documents/rockets/
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or a file manager window showing 'upgraded_rocket.ork' located in the 'Documents/rockets' directory.

task_completion.description: Write a material substitution report discussing changes, mass, and apogee
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the contents of 'material_upgrade_report.txt' in ~/Documents/exports/. The text must be at least a few sentences (approx 200 chars) and explicitly mention the material changes, the rocket's mass, and the simulated apogee.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify materials and run simulations
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the OpenRocket graphical interface (component edit windows, simulation tabs) rather than just editing the .ork XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The report contains genuine simulation data derived from the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The mass and apogee values written in the report should match or closely reflect the values visible in the OpenRocket simulation tab screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent wrote an original report rather than just copying the prompt instructions
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in 'material_upgrade_report.txt' should be a coherent summary of the specific changes made and the resulting metrics, not just a copy-paste of the task description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Base design path: /home/ga/Documents/rockets/hpr_upgrade_base.ork. Upgraded design path: /home/ga/Documents/rockets/upgraded_rocket.ork. Report path: /home/ga/Documents/exports/material_upgrade_report.txt. The Nose Cone and Body Tube must be changed to Fiberglass (density >= 1100 kg/m^3). The Fins must be changed to Plywood or Birch (density >= 500 kg/m^3). The report must mention material changes, mass, and simulated apogee.
